notes.eibor: 3.900% (est.) as of July 14 2026; CBUAE base rate 3.65% confirmed July 14; last live EIBOR confirmation July 10 2026; EIBOR-base spread 25bps; 6 consecutive unchanged days (streak began July 9)
notes.brent: July 14 2026: $87.09 (TradingEconomics); Fortune $86.99 corroborates. Flat vs. prior session $87.09. 7th session at elevated Hormuz-crisis levels. +$17.53/bbl vs pre-escalation FRED close $69.56. FRED July 6 $69.56 stale — overridden.
notes.wti: July 14 FXLeaders $79.56 (−$1.28, −1.6% vs prior $80.84). US-Iran interim deal reported — partial war premium removal. FRED July 6 $69.60 stale — overridden. Brent-WTI spread $7.53 (elevated vs normal $2-4).
notes.hormuz: Day 135+ of formal closure. US-Iran interim deal reported July 14 (FXLeaders) — unconfirmed, partial war premium removal. 34 ships/day vs 88 normal (39% throughput). ADNOC Fujairah bypass 1.5 mb/d (~50% UAE export coverage). Interim deal: increased Iranian oil exports possible but Hormuz not yet reopened.
notes.uae_opec_exit: UAE formally exited OPEC+ effective May 1 2026. UAE production 3.8 mb/d (Reuters July 6 2026). Targeting 5 mb/d by 2031.
notes.make_it_in_emirates: AED 180bn industrial procurement opportunities (Make it in the Emirates 2026). 20% conversion = AED 7.2bn/yr additional pace — closes the AED 6.6bn/yr deficit.
notes.fed: June 17 FOMC — 5th consecutive hold; Fed Funds 3.50–3.75%. July 29 FOMC upcoming — ~25% hike probability pre-June CPI. June CPI due July 14 at 12:30 UTC — not yet released at brief generation (11:15 UTC). FEDFUNDS 3.63% (June, 43 days stale — assumed unchanged).
notes.us_cpi: May 2026: 4.20% YoY (FRED stale). June 2026 CPI due July 14 at 12:30 UTC — not yet released at brief generation time (11:15 UTC). Forecast ~4.1–4.2%.
notes.us_10yr: 4.56% (FRED July 10, valid, +2bps from 4.54% July 9). Breakeven 2.26% (FRED July 13, valid, +2bps from 2.24%). Real yield ~2.30%.
notes.opec_plus: OPEC basket price unavailable (scraper null). UAE exited OPEC+ May 2026. UAE production 3.8 mb/d.
notes.operation_300bn: AED 200bn milestone confirmed May 4 2026; 40.1% of gap closed [(200-133)/(300-133)]; AED 100bn remaining; AED 20.0bn/yr pace required; current pace AED 13.4bn/yr; deficit AED 6.6bn/yr; at current pace ~2034 (3 years late); fiscal surplus [A] AED 163.4bn/yr at $87.09 Brent; war-premium uplift [B] AED 89.3bn/yr; post-deal scenario [C] AED 74.1bn/yr
notes.fred_status: FEDFUNDS 3.63% (June, 43 days stale — assumed unchanged); INDPRO 102.65 (May, 74 days stale — background only); DCOILBRENTEU $69.56 (July 6, 8 days stale — overridden by web $87.09 July 14); DCOILWTICO $69.60 (July 6, 8 days stale — web override $79.56 July 14); DGS10 4.56% (July 10, valid); T10YIE 2.26% (July 13, valid); CPIAUCSL May 2026 (74 days stale — June due today 12:30 UTC, not yet released)
notes.petrochemical: AED 1,985mn/yr net feedstock additional cost at $87.09 Brent vs pre-escalation $69.56; gross AED 2,646mn/yr (60% pass-through on AED 17.5bn); ~25% ADNOC offset; UNCHANGED from prior brief (Brent flat session-on-session)
notes.world_bank: All four World Bank AE API calls timed out July 14 (4th consecutive session). Prior state.json baselines used: UAE GDP $552.3bn, industry 44.3% GDP, CPI 1.25%, FDI $45.6bn.
notes.us_iran_deal: FXLeaders July 14 2026 reports US-Iran interim deal removing war premium, allowing increased Iranian oil exports. Unconfirmed. WTI fell $1.28 to $79.56 on this signal. Brent held at $87.09 (Hormuz routing risk still priced in). Monitor Brent-WTI spread compression below $5 as confirmation signal.

claude_md_patches_applied.patch: Every material figure must trace to a shown calculation block or named source. Compute each derived quantity ONCE in a calculation block, label precisely what each represents, and reuse exact numbers everywhere. Three oil-fiscal figures labelled [A], [B], [C] with distinct bases. Key number and headline must be internally consistent.
AED/USD peg transmission chain must appear as one explicit connected arrow-chain sentence in every brief. Non-droppable element of Type A on every run.
Tag every inline use of estimated EIBOR with (est.) directly next to the figure in every scenario line of the calculation block. EIBOR source line must be first line of calculation block.
EIBOR estimation basis must appear as first line of calculation code block. Sources section mandatory with vintage dates. Keep brief body under 3,500 words.
Use **Key number:** (bold, lowercase) and **Watch list — next 72 hours:** (bold). Sources and Methodology mandatory. No YAML frontmatter.
EIBOR estimation chain must be first line of calculation block. Stale FRED series disclosed by name with vintage dates in body AND Methodology. Methodology section mandatory.
Always run all four required calculations in every brief. Corrected Op300bn method: (200-133)/(300-133)=40.1% of gap, not 200/300=66.7%.
